changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage deep tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures spun out as a separate investment firm in 2023 from The Engine, which remains a 501(c)(3) nonprofit incubator/accelerator founded by MIT in 2016. Source [4] is unrelated SpaceX news and provides no information about Engine Ventures. Wikipedia entry notes 'In 2023, the investment fund separated from The Engine and rebranded as the venture capital firm Engine Ventures.' No current fund name, AUM, or named senior partners explicitly confirmed in provided sources.

changesProposed.name: Khosla Ventures

verified.name: Khosla Ventures
verified.thesis: Khosla Ventures is passionate about technology-based economic disruption in many industries. The firm seeks unfair advantages through proprietary and protected technological advances, business model innovations, and unique approaches to markets. The firm believes AI has the power to disrupt numerous economic models and change the way people lead their daily lives.
verified.notes: Keith Rabois returned to the firm as a managing director in February 2024 (source 4). The firm closed $3.1 billion in new capital as of November 2023 across three funds (source 4). AUM figure is from October 2021 per Wikipedia (source 4).
